mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rising rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0%, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand and rising recession probability, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint positive changes in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive forward returns in >50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth signals strengthening manufacturing output and corporate revenue momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative forward returns in >55% of observations during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS changes correlate with negative forward returns in >50% of monthly observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 195.0, accelerating inflation triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings above 195.0 are followed by positive forward returns in >60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in the unemployment rate and the federal funds rate indicate a deteriorating macroeconomic environment with rising borrowing costs and weakening labor demand, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns in >55% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 2.0% confirms robust economic expansion and supports corporate profit growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth above 2.0% is followed by negative forward returns in >50% of quarterly observations.
